named_technique: Axiomatize Bloom-Sisask 2020 (arXiv:2007.03528 Thm 1.1) — close slot-1320 sorry by real-analysis asymptotics
bridge_lemma: Given Bloom-Sisask axiom (exists c, C > 0 with r_3(N) <= C N / (log N)^{1+c} for N >= 2), multiplication by log N yields r_3(N) log N <= C N / (log N)^c which tends to 0 as N grows since c > 0, hence is eventually less than N, closing the slot-1320 sorry rothNumberNat_times_log_eventually_lt in 20-40 lines via Real.tendsto_rpow_atTop, tendsto_log_atTop, Filter.Tendsto.eventually_gt_atTop, and Real.log_pos.
informal_proof_outline: GIVEN: Bloom–Sisask Theorem 1.1 (arXiv:2007.03528) — there exist absolute constants 0 < c, C such that r_3(N) <= C * N / (log N)^{1+c} for all N >= 2. AXIOMATIZE this as `axiom bloom_sisask_roth_bound : ∃ c C : ℝ, 0 < c ∧ 0 < C ∧ ∀ N : ℕ, 2 ≤ N → (rothNumberNat N : ℝ) ≤ C * N / (Real.log N)^(1+c)`. CLOSE the sorry as follows. Step 1: obtain c, C, hc, hC, h_bs from the axiom. Step 2: observe that for N >= 2, Real.log N >= Real.log 2 > 0, hence (Real.log N)^{1+c} > 0 and the bound rewrites as r_3(N) * Real.log N <= C * N * Real.log N / (Real.log N)^{1+c} = C * N / (Real.log N)^c. Step 3: since c > 0, (Real.log N)^c → infinity as N → infinity (Real.tendsto_rpow_atTop and tendsto_log_atTop composed), so C / (Real.log N)^c → 0, i.e., for N >= N_1 (some explicit N_1 depending on c, C), C / (Real.log N)^c < 1. Step 4: at this N_1, r_3(N) * Real.log N <= C * N / (Real.log N)^c = N * (C / (Real.log N)^c) < N * 1 = N. Step 5: pick N_1 := max(2, some bound from Filter.Eventually) and conclude. The Lean machinery needed lives in Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Pow.Asymptotics (tendsto_rpow_atTop, isLittleO_log_rpow_atTop) and Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Log.Basic (Real.log_pos, tendsto_log_atTop). HONEST DISCLOSURE: axiomatizing Bloom–Sisask sets axiomatizes_prior_work=1, so target_resolved=1 is unattainable; the goal is `compiled_no_advance` becoming `compiled_partial` with the axiom isolated AND the slot-1320 sorry eliminated under the axiom. This is the BEST possible outcome given Mathlib's current Roth state.
candidate_lemmas: axiom bloom_sisask_roth_bound : ∃ c C : ℝ, 0 < c ∧ 0 < C ∧ ∀ N : ℕ, 2 ≤ N → (rothNumberNat N : ℝ) ≤ C * N / (Real.log N) ^ (1 + c)
tendsto_log_atTop : Tendsto Real.log Filter.atTop Filter.atTop (Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Log.Basic, line 319)
tendsto_rpow_atTop : 0 < y → Tendsto (fun x => x ^ y) Filter.atTop Filter.atTop (Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Pow.Asymptotics, line 32)
Filter.Tendsto.atTop_mul_const : 0 < c → Tendsto f atTop atTop → Tendsto (fun x => f x * c) atTop atTop
Filter.Tendsto.eventually_gt_atTop : Tendsto f atTop atTop → ∀ a, ∀ᶠ x in atTop, a < f x
div_lt_iff (h : 0 < c) : a / c < b ↔ a < b * c (Mathlib.Order.Field.Basic)
honest_disclaimer: PAIRED-LLM = grok-4-fast-reasoning + manual Mathlib audit (May 31 2026 MEGA-9 consultation). VERIFIED FACTS: (1) Mathlib has only the qualitative rothNumberNat_isLittleO_id (file Mathlib/Combinatorics/Additive/Corner/Roth.lean), giving r_3(N) = o(N). cornersTheoremBound is tower-type and CANNOT yield r_3(N) * log N < N — the slot-1320 ARISTOTLE_SUMMARY.md explicitly notes this. (2) Mathlib has NO Bohr sets, NO Gowers norms, NO Croot–Sisask almost-periodicity, NO density-increment machinery on Z/NZ. (3) Bloom–Sisask 2020 (arXiv:2007.03528) is the FIRST published bound to break the log barrier — Sanders 2011, Bloom 2016, Schoen 2021 all give only (log log N)^{O(1)}/(log N) factors which are INSUFFICIENT (multiplying by log N produces (log log N)^{O(1)} growth, not bounded). Bourgain 1999 r_3(N) << N(loglog/logN)^{1/2} is INSUFFICIENT. Heath-Brown 1987 r_3(N) << N(loglog)^c/sqrt(logN) is INSUFFICIENT. (4) Bloom–Sisask 2023 (arXiv:2305.05400) sharpens to c=1/9 — overkill but available as a higher-strength axiom. Kelley–Meka 2023 (arXiv:2302.07211) is exp(-c(logN)^{1/12}) — also overkill. WHAT THIS LANE CAN: surface the precise minimal axiom (Bloom–Sisask Theorem 1.1), give Aristotle the lemma structure for the real-analysis reduction in ~20–40 lines, and route the slot-1320 sorry to a single axiom rather than a sorry. WHAT THIS LANE CANNOT: prove Bloom–Sisask in Lean (the prerequisite stack — Bohr sets, Gowers, Croot–Sisask — is a multi-year formalization project NOT in Mathlib as of May 2026, confirmed via web search). BAYESIAN PRIORS (MEGA-9 adversarial Round 5): P(target_resolved=1 within 72h, full sorry-free compile without axiom) ~ 0.02; P(compiled_partial with axiom isolated and slot-1320 sorry eliminated under the axiom) ~ 0.40; P(compiled_no_advance, Aristotle bounces on the asymptotic chasing or refuses the axiom) ~ 0.50; P(novel partial: Aristotle finds a sharper Mathlib argument we missed) ~ 0.08. This represents the BEST KNOWN lever for the slot-1320 sorry: a precisely-cited weakest-sufficient axiom paired with elementary real-analysis. Per CLAUDE.md rule 10: HAVE FAITH IN ARISTOTLE.
